title: capnophyllum peregrinum
intro: Capnophyllum peregrinum, commonly known as Wild Valerian or Chinese Valerian, is a plant species in the Valerianaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, as well as its ability to relieve insomnia and other sleep disorders. The therapeutic actions of Capnophyllum peregrinum include sedative and anxiolytic effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as valepotriates, isovalerenic acid, and sesquiterpenes. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and therapeutic actions.
side_effects: Capnophyllum peregrinum can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can lead to more severe consequences, including vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, improper use of the plant has been linked to allergic reactions, such as itching, swelling, and difficulty breathing. If not used under the guidance of a medical professional, the side effects of Capnophyllum peregrinum can be unpredictable and potentially life-threatening.
